patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH] mm: memcontrol: propagate NMI slab stats to memcg vmstats

version_history.subject: [PATCH] mm: memcontrol: propagate NMI slab stats to memcg vmstats

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the memcontrol code where NMI slab stats are not propagated to memcg vmstats, leading to inconsistent statistics. The fix involves modifying the flush_nmi_stats function to update both per-node lruvec_stats and memcg-level vmstats.

review_comments.author: Johannes Weiner
review_comments.summary: Acknowledged the patch without any issues or concerns.
review_comments.raw_body:
On Mon, May 18, 2026 at 10:28:19AM +0200, Alexandre Ghiti wrote:
> flush_nmi_stats() drains per-node NMI slab atomics into the per-node
> lruvec_stats, but does not propagate them to the memcg-level vmstats.
> 
> For non NMI case, account_slab_nmi_safe() calls mod_memcg_lruvec_state()
> which updates both per-node lruvec_stats and memcg-level vmstats, so
> flush_nmi_stats() needs to flush to per-node lruvec_stats as well as
> memcg-level vmstats.
> 
> So fix this by flushing to the memcg-level vmstats for NMI too.
> 
> Fixes: 940b01fc8dc1 ("memcg: nmi safe memcg stats for specific archs")
> Cc: stable@vger.kernel.org
> Acked-by: Shakeel Butt <shakeel.butt@linux.dev>
> Signed-off-by: Alexandre Ghiti <alex@ghiti.fr>

Acked-by: Johannes Weiner <hannes@cmpxchg.org>

review_comments.author: Harry Yoo
review_comments.summary: Approved the patch and added a Reviewed-by tag.
review_comments.raw_body:
On 5/18/26 5:28 PM, Alexandre Ghiti wrote:
> flush_nmi_stats() drains per-node NMI slab atomics into the per-node
> lruvec_stats, but does not propagate them to the memcg-level vmstats.
> 
> For non NMI case, account_slab_nmi_safe() calls mod_memcg_lruvec_state()
> which updates both per-node lruvec_stats and memcg-level vmstats, so
> flush_nmi_stats() needs to flush to per-node lruvec_stats as well as
> memcg-level vmstats.
> 
> So fix this by flushing to the memcg-level vmstats for NMI too.
> 
> Fixes: 940b01fc8dc1 ("memcg: nmi safe memcg stats for specific archs")
> Cc: stable@vger.kernel.org
> Acked-by: Shakeel Butt <shakeel.butt@linux.dev>
> Signed-off-by: Alexandre Ghiti <alex@ghiti.fr>
> ---

Looks good to me,
Reviewed-by: Harry Yoo (Oracle) <harry@kernel.org>

-- 
Cheers,
Harry / Hyeonggon

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH 2/2] btrfs: test qgroup deletion races with squota writes

version_history.subject: [PATCH 2/2] btrfs: test qgroup deletion races with squota writes

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch adds four test cases to the Btrfs file system to detect and prevent various race conditions that can occur when deleting a qgroup (quota group) while writing quotas. The tests cover scenarios where an extent's owner reference outlives its subvolume, leading to potential underflows or transaction aborts. The patch aims to ensure correct quota accounting by rejecting qgroup deletion requests in these situations.

review_comments.author: Anand Jain
review_comments.summary: The reviewer noted that the transaction commit could race with the testcase, and suggested checking for kernel fixes XXXXXXXXXXXX and 0c309d66dacd to resolve the issue.
review_comments.raw_body:
It appears that transaction commit could race with the testcase.


--------
SECTION       -- gen
FSTYP         -- btrfs
PLATFORM      -- Linux/x86_64 vm1 7.1.0-rc3-gf5d9f327e4b2 #14 SMP
PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Sun May 17 21:25:06 +08 2026
MKFS_OPTIONS  -- -f /dev/vde
MOUNT_OPTIONS -- -o context=system_u:object_r:root_t:s0 /dev/vde
/mnt/scratch

btrfs/348    10s ... - output mismatch (see
/Volumes/work/ws/fstests/results//gen/btrfs/348.out.bad)
    --- tests/btrfs/348.out	2026-05-18 14:04:31.427635000 +0800
    +++ /Volumes/work/ws/fstests/results//gen/btrfs/348.out.bad
2026-05-18 17:13:33.000000000 +0800
    @@ -5,6 +5,4 @@
     ERROR: unable to destroy quota group: Device or resource busy
     ERROR: unable to create quota group: Invalid argument
     same txn destroy race
    -ERROR: unable to destroy quota group: Device or resource busy
     pre-existing data destroy
    -ERROR: unable to destroy quota group: Device or resource busy
    ...
    (Run 'diff -u /Volumes/work/ws/fstests/tests/btrfs/348.out
/Volumes/work/ws/fstests/results//gen/btrfs/348.out.bad'  to see the
entire di)

HINT: You _MAY_ be missing kernel fix:
      XXXXXXXXXXXX btrfs: check for subvolume before deleting squota qgroup

HINT: You _MAY_ be missing kernel fix:
      0c309d66dacd btrfs: forbid creating subvol qgroups

Ran: btrfs/348
Failures: btrfs/348
Failed 1 of 1 tests


Thanks, Anand







On 13/5/26 08:43, Boris Burkov wrote:

review_comments.author: Boris Burkov (author)
review_comments.summary:
Author acknowledged a test failure due to an unfixed kernel issue, but did not commit to revising the patch.

Author Boris Burkov responded to a question about which kernel branch the reviewer was running on and whether they had applied a specific patch (404bf5a24f2e) that fixes a critical issue related to squota qgroup deletion. He provided evidence from git logs showing that this patch is not present in the 7.1-rc3 tag but is included in the btrfs/for-next branch.
review_comments.raw_body:
Thank you for running the test and reporting the failure, sorry for the
inconvenience.

---

What kernel branch are you running this on? I couldn't find the sha
f5d9f327e4b2, but I took a look at linus's 7.1-rc3 tag and the fixes
aren't there:

$ git ql v7.1-rc3 fs/btrfs/qgroup.c
e0a85137a882 btrfs: avoid GFP_ATOMIC allocations in qgroup free paths
9a0448847310 btrfs: unexport btrfs_qgroup_reserve_meta()
534c0adacdeb btrfs: collapse __btrfs_qgroup_reserve_meta() into btrfs_qgroup_reserve_meta_prealloc()
5adf3f32c0b3 btrfs: collapse __btrfs_qgroup_free_meta() into btrfs_qgroup_free_meta_prealloc()

vs.

$ git ql btrfs/for-next fs/btrfs/qgroup.c
5c3eb7a1bce0 btrfs: swallow btrfs_record_squota_delta() ENOENT
5e4ccd2a4e70 btrfs: clamp to avoid squota underflow
ed2bb38054d9 btrfs: fix squota accounting during enable generation
404bf5a24f2e btrfs: check for subvolume before deleting squota qgroup
452a1a22e98b btrfs: add missing unlikely to if branches leading to a DEBUG_WARN()
e0a85137a882 btrfs: avoid GFP_ATOMIC allocations in qgroup free paths
9a0448847310 btrfs: unexport btrfs_qgroup_reserve_meta()

I believe that:
404bf5a24f2e btrfs: check for subvolume before deleting squota qgroup

is the critical one, as mentioned in the test's "You _MAY_ be missing
kernel fix" section. Do you have that patch?

If not, I think that is the most likely explanation.

Thanks,
Boris

review_comments.author: Anand Jain
review_comments.summary:
The reviewer confirmed that the required btrfs squota patches were present in the kernel and verified that the test case passes after a fresh restart, indicating that the issue was likely due to a messed-up VM image.

The reviewer asked if the year in the copyright notice should be updated from 2024 to 2026.
review_comments.raw_body:
Boris,


The 7.1.0-rc3-gf5d9f327e4b2 kernel includes the for-next tree
plus my latest f_fsid kernel patches.


I double-checked and the required btrfs squota patches were
indeed present:

$ git log --oneline fs/btrfs | grep "check for subvolume before"
404bf5a24f2e btrfs: check for subvolume before deleting squota qgroup
$ git log --oneline fs/btrfs | grep "forbid creating subvol qgroups"
0c309d66dacd btrfs: forbid creating subvol qgroups

Possibly the issue was likely my messed-up VM image from
SELinux + fanotifywait troubleshooting last evening.

After a fresh restart, btrfs/348 passes:

$ ./check -s gen btrfs/348
...
Ran: btrfs/348
Passed all 1 tests

Sorry for the noise!.

Looks good.


Reviewed-by: Anand Jain <asj@kernel.org>

A small nit (which can be fixed during merge).

---

Do you need this to be   `s/2024/2026/`  ?

Thanks.

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH 1/2] btrfs: inline enable_quota helper in test 301

version_history.subject: [PATCH 1/2] btrfs: inline enable_quota helper in test 301

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch simplifies the btrfs test suite by inlining two calls to enable quota functionality, eliminating a helper function and reducing unnecessary complexity.

review_comments.author: Zorro Lang
review_comments.summary:
Reviewer Zorro Lang expressed amusement at the patch title, which jokingly suggested that fstests might delete itself during execution.

Reviewer noted that this patch is unrelated to the second part of the submission and suggested reviewing them separately.
review_comments.raw_body:
Hahah, to be honest, the title '[PATCH 0/2] squota delete fstests' gave me
a minor heart attack. I thought it was a critical bug report about fstests
accidentally deleting itself while running :-D

---

This change doesn't seem related to patch 2/2. I think we can review
these 2 patches separately.

For this patch, I'm fine with or without it. If the btrfs list prefers
to have it, or move it to be a common helper, I'm good with that.

Thanks,
Zorro

review_comments.author: Boris Burkov (author)
review_comments.summary: Author acknowledged that his original patch submission was unnecessary as a series and agreed it could be sent separately.
review_comments.raw_body:
I pulled this into a separate patch based on Filipe's feedback on the
old version I failed to get in. Agreed it doesn't need to be a series,
just felt related enough to send in a batch, due to the context.

Thanks for taking a look,
Boris

review_comments.author: Zorro Lang
review_comments.summary: Reviewer Zorro Lang noted that since the two patches are independent, one can be merged first as long as it receives an Ack, even if the other patch is still being questioned.
review_comments.raw_body:
Don't worry. What I mean is that as long as one of these two patches gets an
Ack first, even if the other is still being questioned, we can merge it first.
After all, they are independent of each other.

review_comments.author: Anand Jain
review_comments.summary: Reviewer Anand Jain found the patch acceptable and approved it, giving a Reviewed-by tag.
review_comments.raw_body:
looks good.

Reviewed-by: Anand Jain <asj@kernel.org>

Thanks.

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH 0/2] squota delete fstests
patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch resurrects a previous attempt to add test cases for edge scenarios involving the deletion of quota groups in Btrfs file systems, incorporating recent bug fixes and feedback.

review_comments.author: Zorro Lang
review_comments.summary:
Reviewer Zorro Lang jokingly expressed concern that the patch title '[PATCH 0/2] squota delete fstests' might be a critical bug report about fstests deleting itself, but it's actually just a test case for squota qgroup deletion.

reviewer questioned the relevance of this patch to the overall submission and suggested reviewing it separately from patch 2/2
review_comments.raw_body:
Hahah, to be honest, the title '[PATCH 0/2] squota delete fstests' gave me
a minor heart attack. I thought it was a critical bug report about fstests
accidentally deleting itself while running :-D

---

This change doesn't seem related to patch 2/2. I think we can review
these 2 patches separately.

For this patch, I'm fine with or without it. If the btrfs list prefers
to have it, or move it to be a common helper, I'm good with that.

Thanks,
Zorro

review_comments.author: Boris Burkov (author)
review_comments.summary: Author acknowledged that Filipe's feedback was correct and agreed to split the patches into separate submissions, no fix planned but will follow original intent
review_comments.raw_body:
I pulled this into a separate patch based on Filipe's feedback on the
old version I failed to get in. Agreed it doesn't need to be a series,
just felt related enough to send in a batch, due to the context.

Thanks for taking a look,
Boris

review_comments.author: Zorro Lang
review_comments.summary: reviewer suggested merging one patch independently of the other as long as one has an Ack, noting that the patches are not dependent on each other
review_comments.raw_body:
Don't worry. What I mean is that as long as one of these two patches gets an
Ack first, even if the other is still being questioned, we can merge it first.
After all, they are independent of each other.

Reviewer Anand Jain asked if the patch needs a trivial text substitution, specifically replacing '2024' with '2026', and thanked the submitter.
